behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is mainly a handoff/wiring behavior (classifier -> request builder -> API client), not pure label logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network requirement is stated; a stub client can verify outbound payload content. LLM-DEP: none (does not depend on model judgment). Rejected higher level (System) because behavior text only requires verifying request contents, not real external response contracts or external side-effect readback.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the module with a test double API client, invoke classify() with a known taxonomy, and assert the actual dispatched payload contains every taxonomy label exactly as required.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is deterministic local mapping/guard logic on returned label value. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none for this check because the test input is a fixed raw label string. No additional reality signal is needed beyond unit scope.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/decision function with an out-of-taxonomy label and assert the final output is "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: explicitly scoped to normalization determinism for identical inputs, which is pure local logic. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none because raw response is fixed and repeated. Higher levels do not add reality signal for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization repeatedly on the same raw response fixture and assert identical final label each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency of returned labels relative to provider responses. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real model/provider outputs across runs; stubs hide this. REAL-INFRA is also involved, but the dominant risk is model/output non-determinism, so Agentic is the lowest sufficient level. Rejected Workflow because this is a single agentic decision per call, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute N real classification calls for the same document/taxonomy, record raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label changes only co-occur with provider-response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: budget enforcement often spans config loading, tokenizer/counting utility, and dispatch gate. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS needed; can be validated with in-process wiring. LLM-DEP: none; this is pre-dispatch logic. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is specifically "before dispatch" and policy enforcement, not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low token budget, invoke classify() with over-budget input, and assert module applies truncate/reject policy and only dispatches requests that satisfy the limit.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Requires provider-reported usage from real AI API execution; mocks can mask tokenizer/accounting differences. LLM-DEP default applies because execution path depends on real AI/ML API behavior, even with deterministic assertion. Also has System aspects (real external contract), but Agentic is safer as the required check level here. Rejected Workflow because only one model call path is being validated.
behaviors.test_description: Run real API calls on representative documents, read provider usage metadata from actual responses, and assert reported total tokens do not exceed configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: retry behavior depends on interaction among API adapter, backoff policy, and caller-facing error handling. REAL-INFRA: real network is not strictly required; a deterministic stub can emit rate-limit errors and success. LLM-DEP: none (tests retry orchestration, not model judgment). Rejected System because behavior text does not require validating real provider contract variance; retry logic can be reliably exercised in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Use a provider stub that returns rate-limit signal then success; assert module waits per policy, retries, and ultimately returns a successful label instead of surfacing an error.
